rulings.Case Title: ANEESH GUPTA VS. BESS DAUBENMIRE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion And Motion Of Plaintiff Aneesh Gupta For Entry Of Judgment Against Defendant Lawrence Choy Pursuant To Ccp 664.6; Request For Attorneys Fees Against Defendant Lawrence Choy
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 19, 2019 line 1. PLAINTIFF ANEESH GUPTA Notice And Motion And Motion Of Plaintiff Aneesh Gupta For Entry Of Judgment Against Defendant Lawrence Choy Pursuant To Ccp 664.6; Request For Attorneys Fees Against Defendant Lawrence Choy DENIED. Action voluntarily dismissed on April 28, 2017. Nothing in the record or in the declaration submitted in support of this motion indicates that the parties (not their attorneys) made a request to retain jurisdiction. Mesa RHF Partners v. City of Los Angeles 2019 WL 1416925. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: KATY CHMURA VS. LAWRENCE CHOY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Plaintiff Katy Chmura For Entry Of Judgment Against Lawrence Choy Pursuant To Ccp 664.6; Request For Attorneys Fees Against Defendant Choy
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 19, 2019 line 2. PLAINTIFF KATY CHMURA Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Plaintiff Katy Chmura For Entry Of Judgment Against Lawrence Choy Pursuant To Ccp 664.6; Request For Attorneys Fees Against Defendant Choy DENIED without prejudice. No evidence of compliance with paragraphs 9 and 10 of the Settlement agreement re: Bankruptcy Court approval provided. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: DOMINIC YAM VS. GLORIA YAM ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 19, 2019 line 5. DEFENDANT GLORIA YAM, CHARLES KIM DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend First, Third, and Fourth causes of action as to both Defendants. Plaintiff has not pled any allegations that Charles Kim was party to any oral agreement, made any promise to Dominic. Plaintiff fails to allege enough facts that either Defendant obtained a specific benefit through fraud or for wrongful use. Demurrer is also SUSTAINED with leave to amend as to Second cause of action. Plaintiff has not alleged facts demonstrating the applicability of promissory estoppel. Plaintiff may, if Plaintiff can do so in good faith, allege additional causes of action based on new or developing facts. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK MALATACK ET AL VS. COBALT HOLDINGS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 19, 2019 line 8. DEFENDANT STEPHANIE MATES, SEQUOIA REAL ESTATE, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend First and Second causes of action. Each element in a cause of action for fraud or negligent misrepresentation must be factually and specifically pled. (Cadlo v. Owens-Illinois, Inc. (2004) 125 Cal.App.4th 513, 519) = (501/CFH)
